stories.title: Mason residents raise cash to fight proposed data center
stories.dek: A local group in Ingham County is hosting a fundraiser to push back against a data center project. Residents worry about noise, power strain and lost farmland near their homes. The effort shows growing organized opposition in mid-Michigan communities facing similar proposals.
stories.source_title: Mason group holds fundraiser to oppose data center development - WILX

stories.title: Whitmer tells data centers they must follow new state growth rules
stories.dek: The governor unveiled the Michigan Affordable and Responsible Growth Action Plan, which sets siting, power, and water standards for future data centers. Companies that refuse to comply risk losing state incentives. The plan arrives amid rising local pushback over utility bills and farmland loss.
stories.source_title: Gov. Whitmer launches Michigan Affordable and Responsible Growth Action Plan, calls on data centers to comply - Big Rapids Daily News

stories.title: Dowagiac residents sue hyperscale data center over nonstop noise
stories.dek: A lawsuit filed in Cass County claims the Dowagiac data center is generating constant, disruptive noise that is harming nearby homes. Residents say the facility has already altered daily life in the small southwest Michigan community. The case puts a spotlight on how local governments approve large projects without strong noise protections.
stories.source_title: Dowagiac data center noise sparks lawsuit - Detroit Free Press

stories.title: Vicksburg residents rally against planned data-center projects
stories.dek: Dozens turned out for a peaceful protest highlighting concerns over noise, traffic and strain on local utilities. The demonstration is the latest sign of growing pushback in Kalamazoo County townships. Organizers say more actions are planned if proposals advance.
stories.source_title: Locals gather for a peaceful protest against data centers - Vicksburg Daily News

stories.title: House Speaker stalls Andrews data-center guardrail bill
stories.dek: Legislation that would set statewide rules on data-center siting, power use and tax breaks remains bottled up. Sponsors say the delay leaves townships without tools to manage rapid growth. The bill had bipartisan support before the hold.
stories.source_title: Andrews: data center guardrail legislation being held up by House Speaker - Moody on the Market

stories.title: Utility faces questions on power, water for west Michigan's first major AI campus
stories.dek: The utility will explain how it will serve a large Microsoft AI campus in Kent County at a public hearing this week. Neighbors are already questioning added strain on the local grid and water supply. The project would be one of the first major AI facilities in west Michigan.
stories.source_title: Consumers Energy on Microsoft AI data center in Gaines Township ahead of public hearing - MSN

stories.title: Augusta Twp voters face data center referendum after heated campaign
stories.dek: Residents will decide next month whether to allow a large data center project in Augusta Township. The vote comes after weeks of protests and warnings from locals about traffic, noise and power strain near Ann Arbor. A resident urged officials to ignore social media pressure during the final stretch.
stories.source_title: Augusta Township data center battle reaches the ballot next month: ‘Stay off social media,’ resident tells officials - Planet Detroit

stories.title: Warren Freezes New Data Centers After Storms Down 60+ Power Lines
stories.dek: Council approved the temporary moratorium after July storms exposed the strain on Warren's aging grid. City attorneys will now draft the pause while zoning rules are revised.

stories.title: Saline Township Locals Resist $16B Stargate Data Center Over Water and Farmland
stories.dek: Residents of the 2,400-person farming community say the project already under construction will drain water supplies, raise utility costs and erase rural character.

stories.title: Oracle wins unlimited tax break on $43B Ann Arbor-area campus
stories.dek: Saline Township dropped its $4.8B cap on incentives for the Oracle-OpenAI project. Residents now face decades of lost property-tax revenue with no upper limit on the deal. The reversal hands the company a blank check paid for by local taxpayers.
stories.source_title: Township changes course, nixing cap on tax break for $43B Oracle, OpenAI data center near Ann Arbor - MLive.com

stories.title: Township removes cap on Oracle property tax exemption, risking school and road funding
stories.dek: Saline Township lifted the dollar cap on a proposed tax abatement for Oracle's massive data-center project. Residents could see decades of lost property-tax revenue that would otherwise fund schools and roads. The change shifts the financial stakes for Washtenaw County taxpayers.
stories.source_title: Saline Township Reverses $4.8 Billion Limit on Oracle Tax Break - The Sun Times News

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.
